comment: 58yo perimenopausal female on continuous-combined HRT (estradiol 1 mg + medroxyprogesterone 2.5 mg daily) for 6 years for severe vasomotor symptoms after surgical menopause. Intact uterus. No current cancer diagnosis. IARC Group 1 carcinogen (combined menopausal therapy) — duration ≥5y triggers prevention surveillance. Engine fires RF-IATROGENIC-COMBINED-HRT-PREVENTION via continuous-combined ≥5y branch and routes to PreventionPlan: standard = shared-decision review of risk-benefit (vasomotor benefit vs +1.2-1.7x breast cancer signal + VTE / cardiovascular axis), lowest-effective-dose + shortest-duration principle per NAMS / ACOG, USPSTF breast screening cadence, symptom-prompted endometrial workup; alternative = guideline-driven taper-and-discontinue plan with non-hormonal vasomotor management (SSRIs, gabapentin, lifestyle). Validates v0.3 iatrogenic HRT prevention pilot.
